issues.location: STEP 4d (ArmorOrn fork) vs. source lines 6151-6193 (Install) and 6185 (&MyHook)
issues.problem: The manifest says bring ArmorOrn::Install() "verbatim (prologue 6A FF 68, 7-byte steal)" while DROPPING MyHook. But Install() hard-references &MyHook at line 6185: `*reinterpret_cast<DWORD*>(p + 1) = static_cast<DWORD>(reinterpret_cast<DWORD>(&MyHook) - (target + 5));` — the whole point of Install() is to redirect FUN_00594E50 to MyHook. Copying Install() verbatim while dropping MyHook is a dangling symbol => does NOT compile. (And functionally: a verbatim Install() would actually hook the engine and route every armor repaint through a MyHook that the manifest deleted.)
issues.fix: Do NOT bring Install() verbatim. Take the manifest's stated `write_fresh` alternative as the PRIMARY path: write a tiny `namespace ArmorOrn { static FnArmorApply_t g_orig=nullptr; void PreviewApply(...){...}; void Init(){ g_orig = reinterpret_cast<FnArmorApply_t>(Resolve(Off::FnArmorApply)); } }` that resolves the un-hooked FUN_00594E50 address directly into g_orig (no trampoline, no MyHook, no patch). PreviewApply already calls g_orig only — so a direct resolve fully satisfies the preview path. Call ArmorOrn::Init() from InitThread. This drops the prologue check, the VirtualAlloc/Protect trampoline, AND the MyHook/g_GetItem dependencies in one move.

issues.location: STEP 4d 'Install (6151-6193, verbatim)' and 'g_orig/g_installed globals (6006-6014)' vs. source line 6153
issues.problem: Even setting MyHook aside, Install() at line 6153 references g_GetItem and Off::FnGetItem: `if (!g_GetItem) g_GetItem = reinterpret_cast<FnGetItem_t>(Resolve(Off::FnGetItem));`. The manifest explicitly drops g_GetItem / FnGetItem_t / Off::FnGetItem (STEP 2b: "FnGetItem/GblLocalPC are NOT needed"; STEP 4d DROP list includes FnGetItem_t/g_GetItem). Verbatim Install() therefore dangles on g_GetItem, Off::FnGetItem, and the FnGetItem_t typedef => compile error. Separately, the cited bring-range 6006-6014 itself INCLUDES the FnGetItem_t typedef (6010) and g_GetItem global (6013) the manifest says to drop, so the range and the disposition contradict each other.
issues.fix: Use the write_fresh ArmorOrn from the previous fix (no Install(), so no g_GetItem reference). If instead you insist on a real hook, you MUST also bring g_GetItem + FnGetItem_t + Off::FnGetItem and delete only the LookForRenderSlot worn-walk body lines from Install()'s reach — but that pulls back in exactly what the manifest tried to drop. The clean resolution is write_fresh.

issues.location: STEP 1c InitThread ("exactly these three steps") vs. STEP 4d ("call ArmorOrn::Install() from InitThread — add a 4th line")
issues.problem: STEP 1c states InitThread 'collapses to exactly these three steps' (ResolveBase + InstallHook_DspChat + InstallHook_PwDraw) and shows only those. STEP 4d then says to add a 4th line that installs/initializes ArmorOrn so g_orig resolves before the first preview. These contradict. If a builder follows 1c literally, ArmorOrn::g_orig stays null, MktRenderClone(ps==1/9) and the plain-clone repaint branch silently no-op (PreviewApply returns early on !g_orig) — robe and full-suit armor previews render bare. Not a compile error, but a behavior gap (bootstrap step missing).
issues.fix: Make InitThread FOUR steps: ResolveBase(); ArmorOrn::Init(); InstallHook_DspChat(); InstallHook_PwDraw();. Resolve ArmorOrn first (it only needs g_baseAddr, set by ResolveBase) so g_orig is live before any PwDraw tick can fire MktShowPreview.

issues.location: STEP 2b Off:: table — FnViewportSetModel (485), FnSetWeaponSlot (508), FnAppearanceApply (514), GblEngine (517)
issues.problem: These four constants are listed as 'needed by preview path', but no copied marketplace unit references them. The shipped preview path (MktShowPreview/MktRenderClone/MktRenderCloneBareRebuild, src 4796-4964) only uses GblCDisplay, FnCreatePlayerActor, FnDisplayActorCleanup, GblPreviewSubject, and FnPaperDollPreview (+ ArmorOrn::PreviewApply). FnViewportSetModel/FnSetWeaponSlot/FnAppearanceApply/GblEngine belong to an earlier, abandoned preview approach. They are unused constexpr DWORDs — harmless (no compile/link error), but the 'needed by' justification is false and they bloat the offset header.
issues.fix: Drop these four from the trimmed Off namespace (or keep them but correct the note to 'unused / legacy preview path'). Closure is unaffected either way; FnPaperDollPreview, FnCreatePlayerActor, FnDisplayActorCleanup, GblCDisplay, GblPreviewSubject are the only preview offsets actually referenced.

issues.problem: The manifest's DllMain adds `DisableThreadLibraryCalls(h)` and names the HMODULE param `h`; the source DllMain (6360) takes an unnamed HMODULE and does NOT call DisableThreadLibraryCalls. This is an ADDITION, not a faithful mirror as the prose claims ('Mirror the original two-stage boot'). It is benign/arguably an improvement, but it contradicts the 'mirror' wording and means the carve-out is not byte-faithful here.
issues.fix: Either keep DisableThreadLibraryCalls (fine — correct for a standalone) and update the prose to say 'mirror + add DisableThreadLibraryCalls', or drop it to match the original exactly. No functional risk; documentation accuracy only.

overall: Verified against C:/BOZ-Client-RoF2/src/dllmain_rof2.cpp. The bulk of the manifest is accurate: every Off:: and MktOff:: constant value matches; the forked-hook line ranges (PwDraw 2010-2106, DspChat 3590-3964) and the marketplace branch span (3810-3843) are correctly identified; all Step 3 mkt copy ranges (MktPopulate 2419-2553, tile helpers 2563-2660, MktItem/globals 2668-2750, MktFillOneTile/TickTiles/GridIndexOf, TickMarketplace 2972-3121, ParseBozMkt 3160-3185, the 3D-preview block 4742-5066) are correctly bounded and self-contained within the copy set; the FnCreateActor_t typedef-only take (1800-1802) and the SendServerCommand/CopyField/Log/Resolve/IsReadable helpers are correct. The closure is essentially correct EXCEPT for ArmorOrn. The one fatal flaw is STEP 4d: bringing ArmorOrn::Install() 'verbatim' while dropping MyHook and g_GetItem leaves two dangling references (&MyHook at src 6185, g_GetItem/Off::FnGetItem at src 6153) => the TU will NOT compile. The manifest's own 'write_fresh' alternative (resolve Off::FnArmorApply directly into g_orig, no hook, no MyHook, no g_GetItem) is the correct path and should be promoted to primary; it also eliminates the contradiction that the cited 6006-6014 bring-range includes the very FnGetItem_t/g_GetItem the manifest says to drop. Secondary: the InitThread step count is internally inconsistent (1c says 'exactly three steps', 4d adds a 4th for ArmorOrn) — without the 4th step g_orig stays null and robe/full-suit previews render bare. Minor: four Off preview constants (FnViewportSetModel/FnSetWeaponSlot/FnAppearanceApply/GblEngine) are listed as 'needed' but are unreferenced legacy (harmless), and DllMain adds DisableThreadLibraryCalls not present in the source (harmless). Fix ArmorOrn via write_fresh + add the 4th InitThread line and the carve-out compiles and preserves marketplace behavior.
